name: Surburban Bus Transportation
recent_news: Active recruiting of drivers (ongoing 2024-2025); no reported M&A activity. Marketing emphasizes 25+ years of operation serving Long Island.
fit_notes: Solid Nassau County, NY footprint with ~25-year operating history under apparent single-family/owner-operator structure (Gunther). Size fits classic tuck-in profile (~$17M est. rev; 100-120 employees). Long Island school bus market is fragmented and Summit-relevant; succession opportunity if Gunther family is the sole principal.

name: ABC Transit Inc
principals: Casey Silverman (spouse; daughter of late Bill Roenigk of W.L. Roenigk Inc.)
districts_or_customers: multiple western PA districts (~25,000 students transported daily across 7 ops locations)
recent_news: Named School Bus Fleet 'Contractor of the Year' 2023. Won Highlands SD contract from W.L. Roenigk in 2019. Ordered first 5 electric buses for 2023-24. Continues active growth via contract wins and tech investment.
fit_notes: High-quality western PA platform — fleet of ~470 is well above target tuck-in scale (more of a regional bolt-on or platform-extension candidate). Owner Silverman is mid-career (low succession signal) and actively growing; unlikely near-term seller but could be aspirational consolidation target. Strong contract base in Pittsburgh metro fits Summit expansion thesis.

name: Liberty Lines Bus Assoc Inc
fleet_size_est: ~324-350 vehicles (transit fleet; minimal/none dedicated school bus)
employees_est: 11 (this entity); ~750 across Liberty Lines Transit umbrella
service_types: transit (Bee-Line bus contract w/ Westchester County)
districts_or_customers: transports high school students in Yonkers/Mount Vernon via transit routes (not dedicated school bus contracts)
recent_news: Long-running Westchester County DOT contract; expanded electric/hybrid bus fleet; engaged with Yonkers Public Schools on workforce development. Not engaged in dedicated school bus contracting.
fit_notes: POOR FIT — Liberty Lines is primarily a municipal fixed-route transit contractor for Westchester County Bee-Line system, not a K-12 school bus operator. The 'Bus Assoc Inc' entity at 475 Saw Mill River Rd is the legacy holding company for Liberty Lines Transit. Some incidental high-school student transport via public transit but no dedicated yellow-bus contracts identified. Recommend deprioritize for Summit's school-bus tuck-in thesis.

name: Baumann & Sons Buses School
owner_or_parent: ABA Transportation Holding Co., Inc. (Baumann family)
principals: Baumann family (historical owners); Maureen Ciaci listed as manager in legacy records
fleet_size_est: historically one of largest on Long Island (post-Atlantic Express bankruptcy absorption 2013); now ~0 (operations ceased)
employees_est: ~900 at peak (laid off April 2020); current operations effectively shuttered
service_types: special-needs (historical via Acme Bus Corp affiliate)
districts_or_customers: Historically: Freeport, Baldwin, Rockville Centre, Hicksville, Northport-East Northport and other Long Island districts
recent_news: Shut down operations April 2020 amid COVID school closures; laid off ~900 drivers. Baumann & Sons Buses, Acme Bus Corp, and ABA Transportation Holding all filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy (E.D.N.Y., proceedings through 2022). Other operators absorbed routes.
fit_notes: NOT ACQUIRABLE — entity ceased operations in 2020 and went through bankruptcy. Any remaining assets/contracts already absorbed by Long Island competitors (e.g., WE Transport, Suffolk Transportation). Remove from active target list; data-source artifact in pilot list.

name: Tri County Transportation
owner_or_parent: Beacon Mobility (acquired 2022; Beacon backed by Audax Private Equity)
principals: Gerard 'Jerry' Tibbott (President; took over from parents in 1992)
recent_news: Acquired by Beacon Mobility in 2022 (announced via Punxsutawney Spirit). Founder-family operator (Tibbotts) sold after ~58 years independent ownership. Now part of Beacon's multi-state platform.
fit_notes: NOT AVAILABLE — already part of Beacon Mobility (Audax-backed competitor PE platform) since 2022. Remove from target list; could be relevant only as competitive intel / market mapping. Confirms Beacon is actively rolling up west-central PA rural school bus operators — a competitive signal for Summit's PA expansion.
